result.stdout:
Description:
  PostgreSQL Data-Tier Application Tools - Extract, compile, and publish PostgreSQL schemas

Usage:
  postgresPacTools [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  extract        Extract database schema to .pgproj.json or SDK-style .csproj project
  publish        Publish schema changes to target database
  script         Generate deployment script without executing
  compile        Compile and validate project dependencies
  deploy-report  Generate report of deployment changes

commands.payload:
Description:
  PostgreSQL Data-Tier Application Tools - Extract, compile, and publish PostgreSQL schemas

Usage:
  postgresPacTools [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  extract        Extract database schema to .pgproj.json or SDK-style .csproj project
  publish        Publish schema changes to target database
  script         Generate deployment script without executing
  compile        Compile and validate project dependencies
  deploy-report  Generate report of deployment changes

result.stdout:
Description:
  Compile and validate project dependencies

Usage:
  postgresPacTools compile [options]

Options:
  -sf, --source-file <source-file> (REQUIRED)  Source .pgproj.json or .csproj file
  -o, --output-path <output-path>              Output file path (default: bin/Debug/net10.0/{DatabaseName}.pgpac)
  -of, --output-format <output-format>         Output format: pgpac (default) or json [default: pgpac]
  -v, --verbose                                Show detailed compilation output [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                               Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Compile and validate project dependencies

Usage:
  postgresPacTools compile [options]

Options:
  -sf, --source-file <source-file> (REQUIRED)  Source .pgproj.json or .csproj file
  -o, --output-path <output-path>              Output file path (default: bin/Debug/net10.0/{DatabaseName}.pgpac)
  -of, --output-format <output-format>         Output format: pgpac (default) or json [default: pgpac]
  -v, --verbose                                Show detailed compilation output [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                               Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generate report of deployment changes

Usage:
  postgresPacTools deploy-report [options]

Options:
  -sf, --source-file <source-file> (REQUIRED)                             Source .pgproj.json file
  -tcs, --target-connection-string <target-connection-string> (REQUIRED)  Target PostgreSQL database connection string
  -of, --output-file <output-file> (REQUIRED)                             Path to output report file (JSON)
  -?, -h, --help                                                          Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Generate report of deployment changes

Usage:
  postgresPacTools deploy-report [options]

Options:
  -sf, --source-file <source-file> (REQUIRED)                             Source .pgproj.json file
  -tcs, --target-connection-string <target-connection-string> (REQUIRED)  Target PostgreSQL database connection string
  -of, --output-file <output-file> (REQUIRED)                             Path to output report file (JSON)
  -?, -h, --help                                                          Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Extract database schema to .pgproj.json or SDK-style .csproj project

Usage:
  postgresPacTools extract [options]

Options:
  -scs, --source-connection-string <source-connection-string> (REQUIRED)  Source PostgreSQL database connection string
  -tf, --target-file <target-file> (REQUIRED)                             Path to output file (.pgproj.json for JSON, .csproj for SDK-style project)
  -dn, --database-name <database-name>                                    Database name (overrides connection string)
  -v, --verbose                                                           Show detailed extraction progress [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                                          Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Extract database schema to .pgproj.json or SDK-style .csproj project

Usage:
  postgresPacTools extract [options]

Options:
  -scs, --source-connection-string <source-connection-string> (REQUIRED)  Source PostgreSQL database connection string
  -tf, --target-file <target-file> (REQUIRED)                             Path to output file (.pgproj.json for JSON, .csproj for SDK-style project)
  -dn, --database-name <database-name>                                    Database name (overrides connection string)
  -v, --verbose                                                           Show detailed extraction progress [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                                          Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Publish schema changes to target database

Usage:
  postgresPacTools publish [options]

Options:
  -sf, --source-file <source-file> (REQUIRED)                             Source .pgproj.json file
  -tcs, --target-connection-string <target-connection-string> (REQUIRED)  Target PostgreSQL database connection string
  -v, --variables <variables>                                             SQLCMD variables in format Name=Value
  -dons, --drop-objects-not-in-source                                     Drop objects in target that don't exist in source [default: False]
  --transactional                                                         Execute deployment in a transaction [default: True]
  -?, -h, --help                                                          Show help and usage information

  -v, --variables <variables>                                             SQLCMD variables in format Name=Value
  -dons, --drop-objects-not-in-source                                     Drop objects in target that don't exist in source [default: False]
  --transactional                                                         Execute deployment in a transaction [default: True]
  -?, -h, --help                                                          Show help and usage information

  -tcs, --target-connection-string <target-connection-string> (REQUIRED)  Target PostgreSQL database connection string

  -v, --variables <variables>                                             SQLCMD variables in format Name=Value
  -dons, --drop-objects-not-in-source                                     Drop objects in target that don't exist in source [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                                          Show help and usage information
